items.question: Define unsupervised learning and give two example tasks.
items.answer: Unsupervised learning trains on unlabeled data to discover structure without predicting predefined targets. Example tasks: (1) clustering customers into segments by behavior; (2) PCA to compress high-dimensional embeddings to 2D for visualization.

items.question: How is unsupervised learning different from supervised and self-supervised learning?
items.answer: Supervised uses labeled y and optimizes prediction. Self-supervised constructs surrogate labels from data (masked prediction, contrastive pairs). Classical unsupervised (clustering, PCA, anomaly detection) discovers structure in X without either human labels or constructed pseudo-targets.

items.question: Why can't you rely only on internal metrics like silhouette score to accept a clustering?
items.answer: Silhouette measures compactness/separation in the chosen feature space, not business correctness. High scores can still yield useless segments. Validate with interpretable profiles, stability across seeds, and alignment with KPIs or partial labels.

items.question: A dataset has no labels but you need fraud alerts. Which unsupervised family fits and why?
items.answer: Anomaly detection: learn a model of normal transaction patterns from unlabeled history and flag low-density or high-isolation points for analyst review, prioritizing queue before labeled fraud examples exist for supervised training.
